title: minuartia recurva subsp. bulgarica
intro: Minuartia recurva subsp. bulgarica, also known as the Bulgarian rock-cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and a sprawling habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Bulgarian rock-cress include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, flu, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Bulgarian rock-cress include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
